NewsletterSubscription.title: NewsletterSubscription
NewsletterSubscription.description: Represents a user's subscription to the restaurant's newsletter, capturing their contact information for updates and promotions.

name.description: The full name of the user subscribing to the newsletter.

email.description: The email address of the user subscribing to the newsletter.

subscriptionDate.description: The date and time when the user subscribed to the newsletter.

ComingSoonLead.title: ComingSoonLead
ComingSoonLead.description: Represents a lead captured from the 'Coming Soon' page, including their name, email, and WhatsApp for future contact.

name.description: The full name of the interested lead.

email.description: The email address of the lead.

whatsapp.description: The WhatsApp number of the lead.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the lead was submitted.

definition.description: Stores individual newsletter subscriptions. This collection is designed for public write access (any user can subscribe) and restricted read/list access (only administrators can view the list of subscribers). Includes a unique ID, subscriber name, email, and subscription date. No denormalized authorization fields are required here as creation is public and admin access is role-based from a separate collection.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific newsletter subscription document.

definition.description: Stores leads captured from the 'Coming Soon' page. Public write access is allowed for anyone to submit their interest, but read/list access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific lead document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the OlivaTable application prioritizes simplicity, security, and adherence to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and supporting Query-Able Paths (QAPs). Given the primary requirement for a newsletter subscription form, a single top-level collection, '/newsletter_subscriptions', is recommended.

**Authorization Independence:** Each document within the '/newsletter_subscriptions' collection is entirely self-contained. A new subscription can be created without any dependency on other documents or complex hierarchical relationships. There are no subcollections, and authorization for creating a subscription is public, meaning no `get()` calls are needed to check parent data or user roles. For administrative access to view the subscriptions, authorization will depend on a separate, dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection, external to the newsletter data itself. This completely eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, making security rules atomic and easy to debug.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Public Create:** The design allows for public creation of newsletter subscriptions. A rule like `allow create: if true;` (or with basic data validation) directly enables the client to write a document if it meets schema requirements, without filtering. The entire operation is atomic.
2.  **Admin Read/List:** For administrators to view or list subscriptions, the security rule will be based on their authenticated UID existing in a dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. For example: `allow read, list: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid));`. This rule is a direct access check. If the user is an admin, they can read/list all documents in the collection; if not, the request is denied outright. This ensures that the Firestore rules act as a gatekeeper, not a filter, fully supporting QAPs for both public writes and restricted administrative reads.
